en.help.gettingStarted.palette.title: Command palette & shortcuts
en.help.gettingStarted.palette.content:
Fast navigation

Press Ctrl+K (Windows/Linux) or Cmd+K (macOS) to open the command palette. Search views, AI actions, theme toggles, and pinned favorites. Right-click a command to pin it.

Customize chords under Settings → Shortcuts. Conflicts are highlighted in amber.
en.help.gettingStarted.navigation.title: Sidebar & views overview
en.help.gettingStarted.navigation.content:
Every major view

  • Dashboard — goals, stats, backup quick actions, onboarding tour.
  • Manuscript — three-panel editor with @mentions and #world tags.
  • AI Writing Studio — continue, improve, dialogue, RAG context.
  • Scene Board / Plot Board — canvas, swimlanes, tension curve, AI beat suggestions.
  • Characters, Worlds, Objects, Mind Maps, Interviews — worldbuilding suite (some behind feature flags).
  • Character Graph, Consistency Checker, Critic — analysis tools.
  • Export, Settings, Help — publishing, configuration, learning center.
en.help.gettingStarted.desktop.title: Desktop app (Tauri) & PWA
en.help.gettingStarted.desktop.content:
Where you run WorldScript

Browser / PWA: Install from the browser for offline cache; data stays in IndexedDB.

Desktop (Tauri): Native FS access, Ollama to localhost, optional auto-updater, window state restore, and “open data folder” in Settings → Data.

API keys and manuscripts never leave your device unless you send text to a cloud AI provider.
en.help.writing.writer.title: AI Writing Studio workflow
en.help.writing.writer.content:
Writer view

Select a manuscript section, pick an AI tool, enable RAG context when you want local retrieval, then generate into the scratchpad and insert/replace in the manuscript.

Hybrid fallback chains (Settings → Advanced AI) retry cloud providers automatically.
en.help.writing.sceneboard.title: Plot Board & Scene Board
en.help.writing.sceneboard.content:
Visual plotting

Enable Plot Board v2 under Settings → Experimental flags. Modes: Canvas (pan/zoom, pinch, minimap, SVG connections), Swimlanes (three acts), Timeline.

Long-press a beat card for context actions; connection lines have 44px touch targets. AI can suggest the next beat from manuscript context.
en.help.writing.bookPreview.title: Book preview & progress
en.help.writing.bookPreview.content: Book Preview renders a continuous reading flow. Progress Tracker charts word-count and goal trends over time. Both read from the same manuscript sections as Export.
en.help.worldbuilding.objects.title: Objects & groups inventory
en.help.worldbuilding.objects.content: Turn on Objects & Groups in Settings → Experimental flags. Track props, factions, and grouped entities linked to scenes and characters.
en.help.worldbuilding.mindmaps.title: Mind maps
en.help.worldbuilding.mindmaps.content: Enable Mind Maps in feature flags. SVG canvas with multiple node shapes, links to characters/worlds/objects, and export-friendly structure.
en.help.worldbuilding.interviews.title: Character interviews
en.help.worldbuilding.interviews.content: Enable Character Interviews for archetype-guided AI interview sessions that deepen character voice and motivation.
en.help.worldbuilding.graph.title: Character relationship graph
en.help.worldbuilding.graph.content: The Character Graph visualizes relationships as a force-directed network. Filter by tags, drag nodes, and open dossiers from the graph.
en.help.aiStudio.providers.title: AI providers & keys
en.help.aiStudio.providers.content:
Providers

Configure Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, Grok, Ollama, WebLLM, ONNX, and Transformers.js under Settings → AI and Advanced AI. Keys are encrypted in IndexedDB (AES-256-GCM). Ollama requires the desktop app for localhost access.
en.help.aiStudio.localAi.title: Local AI (WebLLM / ONNX)
en.help.aiStudio.localAi.content: Download models in-browser for offline inference. GPU metrics appear in Advanced AI when available. Smaller ONNX models run on CPU WASM.
en.help.aiStudio.critic.title: AI Critic
en.help.aiStudio.critic.content: The Critic view analyzes pacing, clarity, and tone on selected manuscript passages. Works with the same provider and RAG settings as the Writer.
en.help.aiStudio.plotAi.title: Plot Board AI beats
en.help.aiStudio.plotAi.content: On the Scene Board toolbar, Suggest next beat uses RAG-assembled manuscript context plus your outline to propose the next scene card.
en.help.analysis.consistency.title: Consistency checker
en.help.analysis.consistency.content: Scans manuscript mentions against character/world dossiers and timeline rules. Fix warnings from the dedicated Consistency view.
en.help.analysis.progress.title: Progress & readability
en.help.analysis.progress.content: Dashboard shows Flesch-style readability samples and scene timeline hints. Progress Tracker adds historical charts when goals are set.
en.help.analysis.health.title: Project health score
en.help.analysis.health.content: Enable Project health score in feature flags to see a blended metric on the Dashboard (goals, cast depth, worldbuilding coverage).
en.help.analysis.crossProject.title: Cross-project search
en.help.analysis.crossProject.content: When enabled, search manuscript text across all projects stored locally. Index rebuilds run in the background; results open the target project.
en.help.management.backupDashboard.title: Dashboard backup quick actions
en.help.management.backupDashboard.content: From the Dashboard Backup card: export JSON, import a backup, see the latest snapshot, or jump to Settings → Backup for schedules and encryption.
en.help.management.versionControl.title: Version control panel
en.help.management.versionControl.content: The header Version Control panel lists branches/snapshots for experimentation. Pair with manual snapshots before large edits.
en.help.management.collaboration.title: Collaboration (experimental)
en.help.management.collaboration.content: Yjs-based collaboration panel (when enabled) shares editing sessions over your configured provider. See Settings → Collaboration for WebRTC and room options.
en.help.settingsGuide.overview.title: Settings overview
en.help.settingsGuide.overview.content: Settings are grouped: General, Appearance, Editor, AI, Accessibility, Privacy, Performance, Notifications, Collaboration, Integrations, Backup, Data, Shortcuts, About, and Experimental flags. Use the search box atop Settings to filter categories.
en.help.settingsGuide.ai.title: AI & Advanced AI settings
en.help.settingsGuide.ai.content: Set default provider/model, creativity, hybrid fallback chain, local RAG index mode (lexical vs hybrid), rebuild index, DuckDB analytics toggle, and GPU metrics.
en.help.settingsGuide.accessibility.title: Accessibility hub
en.help.settingsGuide.accessibility.content: Presets for motor comfort, low vision, and cognitive load. Toggles: high contrast, reduced motion (also respects plot-board rAF), large text, screen reader announcements, focus rings, comfortable targets, color-blind modes.
en.help.settingsGuide.backup.title: Backup & data
en.help.settingsGuide.backup.content:
Backup: auto-snapshot interval, encryption passphrase.
Data: JSON export/import, encrypted library ZIP, reset project, snapshot restore, open data folder (desktop).
en.help.settingsGuide.flags.title: All 12 feature flags
en.help.settingsGuide.flags.content:
• Codex auto-tracking
  • Story Bible advanced
  • Research binder
  • Compile wizard
  • Project health score
  • Cross-project search
  • App health panel
  • Plot Board v2
  • DuckDB analytics
  • Objects & groups
  • Mind maps
  • Character interviews

Stored in worldscript-feature-flags localStorage.
en.help.settingsGuide.shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
en.help.settingsGuide.shortcuts.content: Record chords per action; conflicts listed in amber. Defaults include palette, save, new section, search, export, theme toggle.
en.help.docs.architecture.title: Application architecture
en.help.docs.architecture.content:
Stack

React + Vite frontend, Redux Toolkit store, feature slices, service layer for AI/storage/export, lazy-loaded views, Tauri v2 desktop shell.

Heavy modules (DuckDB, force-graph, collaboration/Yjs) load on demand after navigation or flags.
en.help.docs.dataModel.title: Project data model
en.help.docs.dataModel.content: Single StoryProject in Redux: manuscript sections, characters, worlds, scene board layout, plot connections, settings snapshot, goals. Persisted to IndexedDB or Tauri app data via storageService.
en.help.docs.featureFlags.title: Feature flag system
en.help.docs.featureFlags.content: Twelve booleans in featureFlagsSlice, persisted to localStorage, gate sidebar entries and listeners (e.g. DuckDB analytics bootstrap, codex listener).
en.help.docs.ragPipeline.title: RAG & prompt assembly
en.help.docs.ragPipeline.content: Hybrid index: semantic embeddings (MiniLM) + lexical + recency. assembleRAGPrompt injects chunks into Writer/Plot AI calls. Rebuild from Settings → Advanced AI.
en.help.docs.duckdb.title: DuckDB-WASM analytics
en.help.docs.duckdb.content: Optional OPFS-backed DuckDB worker stores 384-dim vectors for analytics dashboards when enableDuckDbAnalytics is on. Listener loads dynamically to keep initial bundle small.
en.help.docs.lazyLoading.title: Lazy loading & bundles
en.help.docs.lazyLoading.content: Views use React.lazy; Plot Board sub-chunks split via Vite manualChunks; aiApi defers provider load; listener middleware dynamic-imports DuckDB/RAG.
en.help.docs.pwaDesktop.title: PWA & desktop packaging
en.help.docs.pwaDesktop.content: PWA service worker caches shell assets. Tauri bundles Rust plugins: fs, dialog, http, shell, updater, optional menu/tray/window-state.
en.help.docs.deployment.title: Deployment options
en.help.docs.deployment.content: Static build via Vite; hosts: Vercel, Cloudflare Pages (build:edge), GitHub Pages. Desktop releases via Tauri CI; signing is maintainer-side.
en.help.docs.privacySecurity.title: Privacy & security model
en.help.docs.privacySecurity.content: Local-first: no cloud account. API keys encrypted at rest. AI requests send only selected text to chosen provider. CSP blocks arbitrary localhost in browser builds.
en.help.proTips.featureFlags.title: Feature flag rollout strategy
en.help.proTips.featureFlags.content: Enable flags one at a time on a snapshot-backed project. Plot Board v2 + DuckDB analytics increase WASM memory — use Performance settings on low-end devices.
en.help.proTips.workflow.title: Recommended writing workflow
en.help.proTips.workflow.content:
1. Outline or template → manuscript
  2. Scene Board for structure
  3. Writer + RAG for drafting
  4. Consistency + Critic passes
  5. Export + JSON backup
en.help.faq.offline.title: Can I work offline?
en.help.faq.offline.content: Yes for writing, snapshots, and local AI models already downloaded. Cloud AI providers need network. PWA install improves offline shell loading.
en.help.faq.providers.title: Which AI provider should I use?
en.help.faq.providers.content:
Gemini — balanced cloud default.
Ollama — desktop localhost privacy.
WebLLM/ONNX — in-browser, no API key.
Enable hybrid fallback to chain providers on errors.

de.help.gettingStarted.palette.title: Befehlspalette & Tastenkürzel
de.help.gettingStarted.palette.content:
Schnelle Navigation

Strg+K bzw. Cmd+K öffnet die Befehlspalette. Suche Views, KI-Aktionen und Themes. Rechtsklick pinnt Favoriten.

Shortcuts unter Einstellungen → Tastenkürzel anpassen.
de.help.gettingStarted.navigation.title: Seitenleiste & Views
de.help.gettingStarted.navigation.content:
Alle Hauptbereiche

  • Dashboard — Ziele, Statistik, Backup-Schnellaktionen.
  • Manuskript — Drei-Panel-Editor mit @- und #-Tags.
  • KI-Schreibstudio — Tools inkl. RAG-Kontext.
  • Szenenbrett / Plot-Board — Canvas, Swimlanes, Spannungskurve.
  • Figuren, Welten, Objekte, Mindmaps, Interviews — Worldbuilding (teilweise per Flag).
  • Figurengraph, Konsistenz, Kritik — Analyse.
  • Export, Einstellungen, Hilfe.
de.help.gettingStarted.desktop.title: Desktop-App (Tauri) & PWA
de.help.gettingStarted.desktop.content:
Laufzeitumgebungen

Browser/PWA: Offline-Cache, Daten in IndexedDB.

Desktop (Tauri): natives FS, Ollama auf localhost, Updater, Fensterstatus, Datenordner öffnen.
de.help.writing.writer.title: KI-Schreibstudio-Ablauf
de.help.writing.writer.content: Abschnitt wählen, KI-Tool nutzen, optional RAG-Kontext, dann im Notizblock bearbeiten und ins Manuskript übernehmen.
de.help.writing.sceneboard.title: Plot-Board & Szenenbrett
de.help.writing.sceneboard.content: Plot Board v2 unter Experimentelle Flags aktivieren. Modi: Canvas (Minimap, Verbindungen), Swimlanes, Timeline. Long-Press auf Karten.
de.help.writing.bookPreview.title: Buchvorschau & Fortschritt
de.help.writing.bookPreview.content: Buchvorschau für Lesefluss; Fortschritt für Ziel- und Wort-Charts.
de.help.worldbuilding.objects.title: Objekte & Gruppen
de.help.worldbuilding.objects.content: Flag Objekte & Gruppen aktivieren für Requisiten und Fraktionen.
de.help.worldbuilding.mindmaps.title: Mindmaps
de.help.worldbuilding.interviews.title: Charakter-Interviews

de.help.aiStudio.providers.title: KI-Anbieter & Schlüssel
de.help.aiStudio.providers.content: Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, Grok, Ollama, WebLLM, ONNX in Einstellungen → KI. Schlüssel verschlüsselt in IndexedDB.
de.help.aiStudio.localAi.title: Lokale KI
de.help.aiStudio.localAi.content: Modelle im Browser laden; GPU-Metriken unter Erweiterte KI.
de.help.aiStudio.critic.title: KI-Kritik
de.help.aiStudio.plotAi.title: Plot-Board-KI-Beats
de.help.aiStudio.plotAi.content: Nächsten Beat vorschlagen nutzt RAG-Kontext aus dem Manuskript.
